root_text.sn46.6:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sākete viharati añjanavane migadāye.
root_text.sn46.6:1.2: Atha kho kuṇḍaliyo paribbājako yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn46.6:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho kuṇḍaliyo paribbājako bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn46.6:1.5: Tassa mayhaṁ, bho gotama, pacchābhattaṁ bhuttapātarāsassa ayamācāro hoti—
root_text.sn46.6:1.6: ārāmena ārāmaṁ uyyānena uyyānaṁ anucaṅkamāmi anuvicarāmi.
root_text.sn46.6:1.7: So tattha passāmi eke samaṇabrāhmaṇe itivādappamokkhānisaṁsañceva kathaṁ kathente upārambhānisaṁsañca:
root_text.sn46.6:2.1: “Katame pana, bho gotama, dhammā bhāvitā bahulīkatā vijjāvimuttiṁ paripūrentī”ti?
root_text.sn46.6:2.2: “Satta kho, kuṇḍaliya, bojjhaṅgā bhāvitā bahulīkatā vijjāvimuttiṁ paripūrentī”ti.
root_text.sn46.6:2.3: “Katame pana, bho gotama, dhammā bhāvitā bahulīkatā satta bojjhaṅge paripūrentī”ti?
root_text.sn46.6:2.4: “Cattāro kho, kuṇḍaliya, satipaṭṭhānā bhāvitā bahulīkatā satta bojjhaṅge paripūrentī”ti.
root_text.sn46.6:2.5: “Katame pana, bho gotama, dhammā bhāvitā, bahulīkatā cattāro satipaṭṭhāne paripūrentī”ti?
root_text.sn46.6:2.6: “Tīṇi kho, kuṇḍaliya, sucaritāni bhāvitāni bahulīkatāni cattāro satipaṭṭhāne paripūrentī”ti.
root_text.sn46.6:2.7: “Katame pana, bho gotama, dhammā bhāvitā bahulīkatā tīṇi sucaritāni paripūrentī”ti?
root_text.sn46.6:2.8: “Indriyasaṁvaro kho, kuṇḍaliya, bhāvito bahulīkato tīṇi sucaritāni paripūretīti.
root_text.sn46.6:3.1: Kathaṁ bhāvito ca, kuṇḍaliya, indriyasaṁvaro kathaṁ bahulīkato tīṇi sucaritāni paripūretīti?
root_text.sn46.6:3.2: Idha, kuṇḍaliya, bhikkhu cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā manāpaṁ nābhijjhati nābhihaṁsati, na rāgaṁ janeti.
root_text.sn46.6:3.3: Tassa ṭhito ca kāyo hoti, ṭhitaṁ cittaṁ ajjhattaṁ susaṇṭhitaṁ suvimuttaṁ.
root_text.sn46.6:3.4: Cakkhunā kho paneva rūpaṁ disvā amanāpaṁ na maṅku hoti appatiṭṭhitacitto adīnamānaso abyāpannacetaso.
root_text.sn46.6:3.5: Tassa ṭhito ca kāyo hoti ṭhitaṁ cittaṁ ajjhattaṁ susaṇṭhitaṁ suvimuttaṁ.
root_text.sn46.6:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, kuṇḍaliya, bhikkhu sotena saddaṁ sutvā …pe…
root_text.sn46.6:4.5: manasā dhammaṁ viññāya manāpaṁ nābhijjhati nābhihaṁsati, na rāgaṁ janeti.
root_text.sn46.6:4.6: Tassa ṭhito ca kāyo hoti, ṭhitaṁ cittaṁ ajjhattaṁ susaṇṭhitaṁ suvimuttaṁ.
root_text.sn46.6:4.7: Manasā kho paneva dhammaṁ viññāya amanāpaṁ na maṅku hoti appatiṭṭhitacitto adīnamānaso abyāpannacetaso.
root_text.sn46.6:4.8: Tassa ṭhito ca kāyo hoti, ṭhitaṁ cittaṁ ajjhattaṁ susaṇṭhitaṁ suvimuttaṁ.
root_text.sn46.6:5.1: Yato kho, kuṇḍaliya, bhikkhuno cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā manāpāmanāpesu rūpesu ṭhito ca kāyo hoti, ṭhitaṁ cittaṁ ajjhattaṁ susaṇṭhitaṁ suvimuttaṁ.
root_text.sn46.6:5.6: manasā dhammaṁ viññāya manāpāmanāpesu dhammesu ṭhito ca kāyo hoti, ṭhitaṁ cittaṁ ajjhattaṁ susaṇṭhitaṁ suvimuttaṁ.
root_text.sn46.6:5.7: Evaṁ bhāvito kho, kuṇḍaliya, indriyasaṁvaro evaṁ bahulīkato tīṇi sucaritāni paripūreti.
root_text.sn46.6:6.1: Kathaṁ bhāvitāni ca, kuṇḍaliya, tīṇi sucaritāni kathaṁ bahulīkatāni cattāro satipaṭṭhāne paripūrenti?
root_text.sn46.6:6.2: Idha, kuṇḍaliya, bhikkhu kāyaduccaritaṁ pahāya kāyasucaritaṁ bhāveti, vacīduccaritaṁ pahāya vacīsucaritaṁ bhāveti, manoduccaritaṁ pahāya manosucaritaṁ bhāveti.
root_text.sn46.6:6.3: Evaṁ bhāvitāni kho, kuṇḍaliya, tīṇi sucaritāni evaṁ bahulīkatāni cattāro satipaṭṭhāne paripūrenti.
root_text.sn46.6:7.1: Kathaṁ bhāvitā ca, kuṇḍaliya, cattāro satipaṭṭhānā kathaṁ bahulīkatā satta bojjhaṅge paripūrenti?
root_text.sn46.6:7.2: Idha, kuṇḍaliya, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn46.6:7.5: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn46.6:7.6: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, kuṇḍaliya, cattāro satipaṭṭhānā evaṁ bahulīkatā satta bojjhaṅge paripūrenti.
root_text.sn46.6:8.1: Kathaṁ bhāvitā ca, kuṇḍaliya, satta bojjhaṅgā kathaṁ bahulīkatā vijjāvimuttiṁ paripūrenti?
root_text.sn46.6:8.2: Idha, kuṇḍaliya, bhikkhu satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ …pe…
root_text.sn46.6:8.3: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
root_text.sn46.6:8.4: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, kuṇḍaliya, satta bojjhaṅgā evaṁ bahulīkatā vijjāvimuttiṁ paripūrentī”ti.
root_text.sn46.6:9.1: Evaṁ vutte, kuṇḍaliyo paribbājako bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn46.6:9.3: Seyyathāpi, bho gotama, nikkujjitaṁ vā ukkujjeyya, paṭicchannaṁ vā vivareyya, mūḷhassa vā maggaṁ ācikkheyya, andhakāre vā telapajjotaṁ dhāreyya: ‘cakkhumanto rūpāni dakkhantī’ti; evameva bhotā gotamena anekapariyāyena dhammo pakāsito.
root_text.sn46.6:9.4: Esāhaṁ bhavantaṁ gotamaṁ saraṇaṁ gacchāmi dhammañca bhikkhusaṅghañca.
root_text.sn46.6:9.5: Upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.sn46.6:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāketa in the deer park at the Añjana Wood.
translation_text.sn46.6:1.2: Then the wanderer Kuṇḍaliya went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn46.6:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn46.6:1.4: “Worthy Gotama, I like to hang around the monasteries and visit the assemblies.
translation_text.sn46.6:1.5: When I’ve finished breakfast, it’s my habit to
translation_text.sn46.6:1.6: wander from monastery to monastery, from park to park.
translation_text.sn46.6:1.7: There I see some ascetics and brahmins speaking for the sake of winning debates and finding fault.
translation_text.sn46.6:1.8: But what benefit does the worthy Gotama live for?”
translation_text.sn46.6:1.9: “The benefit the Realized One lives for, Kuṇḍaliya, is the fruit of knowledge and freedom.”
translation_text.sn46.6:2.1: “But what things must be developed and cultivated in order to fulfill knowledge and freedom?”
translation_text.sn46.6:2.3: “But what things must be developed and cultivated in order to fulfill the seven awakening factors?”
translation_text.sn46.6:2.5: “But what things must be developed and cultivated in order to fulfill the four kinds of mindfulness meditation?”
translation_text.sn46.6:2.7: “But what things must be developed and cultivated in order to fulfill the three kinds of good conduct?”
translation_text.sn46.6:3.1: And Kuṇḍaliya, how is sense restraint developed and cultivated so as to fulfill the three kinds of good conduct?
translation_text.sn46.6:3.2: A mendicant sees an agreeable sight with their eye. They don’t desire it or enjoy it, and they don’t give rise to greed.
translation_text.sn46.6:3.3: Their mind and body are steady internally, well settled and well freed.
translation_text.sn46.6:3.4: But if they see a disagreeable sight they’re not dismayed; their mind isn’t hardened, dejected, or full of ill will.
translation_text.sn46.6:3.5: Their mind and body are steady internally, well settled and well freed.
translation_text.sn46.6:4.1: Furthermore, a mendicant hears an agreeable sound with the ear …
translation_text.sn46.6:4.2: smells an agreeable odor with the nose …
translation_text.sn46.6:4.3: tastes an agreeable flavor with the tongue …
translation_text.sn46.6:4.4: feels an agreeable touch with the body …
translation_text.sn46.6:4.5: knows an agreeable idea with their mind. They don’t desire it or enjoy it, and they don’t give rise to greed.
translation_text.sn46.6:4.6: Their mind and body are steady internally, well settled and well freed.
translation_text.sn46.6:4.7: But if they know a disagreeable idea they’re not dismayed; their mind isn’t hardened, dejected, or full of ill will.
translation_text.sn46.6:4.8: Their mind and body are steady internally, well settled and well freed.
translation_text.sn46.6:5.1: When a mendicant’s mind and body are steady internally, they’re well settled and well freed when it comes to both agreeable and disagreeable sights,
translation_text.sn46.6:5.7: That’s how sense restraint is developed and cultivated so as to fulfill the three kinds of good conduct.
translation_text.sn46.6:6.1: And how are the three kinds of good conduct developed and cultivated so as to fulfill the four kinds of mindfulness meditation?
translation_text.sn46.6:6.2: A mendicant gives up bad conduct by way of body, speech, and mind, and develops good conduct by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.sn46.6:6.3: That’s how the three kinds of good conduct are developed and cultivated so as to fulfill the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn46.6:7.1: And how are the four kinds of mindfulness meditation developed and cultivated so as to fulfill the seven awakening factors?
translation_text.sn46.6:7.2: A mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn46.6:7.3: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn46.6:7.5: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn46.6:7.6: That’s how the four kinds of mindfulness meditation are developed and cultivated so as to fulfill the seven awakening factors.
translation_text.sn46.6:8.1: And how are the seven awakening factors developed and cultivated so as to fulfill knowledge and freedom?
translation_text.sn46.6:8.2: A mendicant develops the awakening factors of mindfulness, investigation of principles, energy, rapture, tranquility, immersion,
translation_text.sn46.6:8.3: and equanimity, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.
translation_text.sn46.6:8.4: That’s how the seven awakening factors are developed and cultivated so as to fulfill knowledge and freedom.”
translation_text.sn46.6:9.1: When he said this, the wanderer Kuṇḍaliya said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn46.6:9.3: As if he were righting the overturned, or revealing the hidden, or pointing out the path to the lost, or lighting a lamp in the dark so people with clear eyes can see what’s there, worthy Gotama has made the teaching clear in many ways.
translation_text.sn46.6:9.4: I go for refuge to the worthy Gotama, to the teaching, and to the mendicant Saṅgha.
translation_text.sn46.6:9.5: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”

reference_text.sn46.6:1.1: csp1ed14.66, csp2ed14.66, dr19.101, ms14S5_399, msdiv187, ndp15.69, pts-vp-pli5.73, vri27.90
